title: Billboard (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 5). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if the base cases are wrong, base the recurrence marks on whether the student applies max(W[n] + opt(n-2), opt(n-1)) correctly using their own base cases.

This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. The Java method is opt(long[] W) with helper opt_helper(int n, long[] W, long[] memo); the Python function is opt(W) with helper opt_helper(n, W, memo). Both are correct.
The recurrence is: opt(n) = max(W[n] + opt(n-2), opt(n-1)), with base cases opt(0) = W[0] and opt(1) = max(W[0], W[1]). Memoization must be used to achieve O(n).
Alternative correct approaches (e.g., bottom-up iterative DP) are also acceptable if they produce the correct result.

rubric_points.name: Memo array allocation and initialisation
rubric_points.target: A memo array of size n+1 is allocated and initialised to -1 as the sentinel for uncached entries.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No memo array is allocated; the recursion recomputes all subproblems on every call, resulting in exponential runtime.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A memo array is present but initialised to 0 instead of -1, making it impossible to distinguish an uncached entry from a subproblem with zero profit; or the array is the wrong size (e.g., n instead of n+1).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: A memo array of size n+1 is allocated and all entries are initialised to -1, correctly serving as the 'not yet computed' sentinel.

rubric_points.name: Base cases
rubric_points.target: opt(0) returns W[0]; opt(1) returns max(W[0], W[1]).

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No base cases are present; the recursion accesses negative indices or runs indefinitely.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Only one base case is present (e.g., n == 0 returns W[0]) but n == 1 is missing, causing an off-by-one access or incorrect result when W has length 2.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Both base cases are correct: n == 0 returns W[0], and n == 1 returns max(W[0], W[1]).

rubric_points.name: Cache check
rubric_points.target: At the start of each helper call, if memo[n] != -1, return memo[n] immediately without recomputation.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No cache check is present; memo is written but never read, so all subproblems are recomputed on every call.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A cache check is present but uses the wrong sentinel (e.g., checks != 0 instead of != -1), causing subproblems with zero profit to be incorrectly recomputed or skipped.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: if memo[n] != -1: return memo[n] at the start of each helper call, ensuring each subproblem is computed at most once.

rubric_points.name: Recurrence and memo store
rubric_points.target: memo[n] = max(W[n] + opt_helper(n-2, ...), opt_helper(n-1, ...)); the result is stored in memo[n] before returning.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No recurrence is applied; the helper returns a constant or does not recurse into smaller subproblems.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A max-based recurrence is present but incorrect: only one branch is computed (always includes or always skips W[n]), or the result is not stored in memo[n] before returning (breaking memoization for callers).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: memo[n] = max(W[n] + opt_helper(n-2, ...), opt_helper(n-1, ...)) is applied correctly; memo[n] is set before returning so subsequent calls to opt_helper(n) use the cached result.

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: opt returns the maximum billboard profit for all valid inputs in O(n) time.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Returns 0 or an incorrect value for most inputs.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Returns correct results for small inputs (n <= 2) but fails for larger inputs due to missing memoization (stack overflow or TLE), or always includes W[n] without considering the skip option.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns the correct maximum profit for all valid inputs (1 <= n <= 1000) in O(n) time.
